rulings.Case Title: KAHN & ASSOCIATES VS. CHRIS GRUWELL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 30, 2023 line 1. CROSS-DEFENDANT UPONOR, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION is OFF CALENDAR. Insufficient notice per CCP 1005 and CCP 1010.6. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MILA POPOV VS. BRYAN KELTNER ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 30, 2023 line 2. DEFENDANTS SANKAET PATHAK AND SYNAPSE FINANCIAL TECH, INC.'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend. The Court notes that the opposition was filed late without leave of Court and without any explanation after 11:00 p.m. on Friday. While the opposition will be considered this time because Defendants addressed it on the merits in the reply, the Court will not consider any filings in violation of the procedural rules in the future. This action was originally brought by Mila Popov, an individual. Contrary to Plaintiff's argument in opposition to the demurrer, that references in the FAC's paragraph 1 are solely for clarification purposes, the caption of the FAC clearly adds two more separately named Plaintiffs: "a trustee of the Mila Popov Living Trust;" and "the manager of Sakiman, LLC." If Popov did not intend to add two more Plaintiffs, Popov must clearly allege that she, as an individual, is the only Plaintiff bringing this action. As such, she must allege, facts not conclusions, supporting her standing, as an individual, to bring each cause of action. If the intent was to bring this action in the name of other plaintiffs, allegations must be made to show that the statute of limitations has not run as to these new plaintiffs. Request for judicial notice is granted. Any amendment made to the claims regarding damages to real and personal property must be made in good faith and must not overlap with any claims regarding damages to real and personal property asserted in the prior action or an explanation must be provided as to the overlap. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: TROY REESE VS. MARTHA ALVAREZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 30, 2023 line 6. DEFENDANT MARTHA ALVAREZ'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. The operative notice failed to advise the tenant of her right to make the demanded payment within three days, excluding Saturdays and Sundays and other judicial holidays. It further appears form the allegations in the Complaint and in opposition that Plaintiff believes that the notice expired on a Saturday, a position not supported by any authority. All other arguments in the demurrer are moot. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
